title: calycobolus cabrae
intro: Calycobolus cabrae is a plant species in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as morning glory. It is traditionally used in West African medicine, particularly in Nigeria, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Calycobolus cabrae is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal use.
